news.headline: Ribbon Acquisition Corp allows separate trading of 5,000,000 IPO units w/ ordinary shares & rights, trading began March 7 on Nasdaq. Contact Odyssey Trust to separate units.
news.summary: Ribbon Acquisition Corp (NASDAQ: RIBBU) recently stated that holders of the 5,000,000 units from its initial public offering now have the option to trade the ordinary shares and rights separately, starting from March 7, 2025. Should the units remain combined, they will keep trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol "RIBBU." The separated ordinary shares and rights are anticipated to be traded on the Nasdaq as "RIBB" and "RIBBR," respectively. Unitholders must get in touch with Odyssey Trust Company, the Company's transfer agent, through their brokers to split the units into ordinary shares and rights.

news.headline: M-tron Industries, Inc. will present at Sidoti Small-Cap Virtual Conference on March 20, 2025. CEO and team to meet investors.
news.summary: M-tron Industries, Inc. (NYSE American: MPTI) revealed that Cameron Pforr, the Interim CEO, alongside other company executives, are set to participate in the Sidoti Small-Cap Virtual Conference on March 19-20, 2025. This event will offer opportunities for investors to engage in one-on-one meetings with Mtron management. The conference will showcase presentations from diverse small-cap firms. Mr. Pforr will discuss Mtron’s ongoing initiatives and strategies during the event.

news.headline: Host Hotels & Resorts (NASDAQ: HST) to report first quarter 2025 financial results on April 30 after market close.
news.summary: Bethesda, Maryland – On March 13, 2025, Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. announced that it will disclose its financial performance for the first quarter of 2025 on Wednesday, April 30, 2025, following the market closure. As the leading lodging real estate investment trust in the country, the company operates under the NASDAQ symbol HST.

news.headline: Zayo, a DigitalBridge portfolio company, to acquire Crown Castle’s Fiber Solutions Business for $4.25 billion.
news.summary: In Boca Raton, Florida, DigitalBridge Group, Inc. (NYSE: DBRG), a prominent digital infrastructure investment firm, revealed that its subsidiary Zayo, acquired with EQT in 2020, has secured a definitive deal to purchase Crown Castle’s Fiber Solutions business. This transaction, valued at around $4.25 billion, aims to enhance Zayo's capacity to provide top-notch connectivity solutions to businesses.

news.headline: DallasNews Corp has sold its Plano, TX North Plant printing facility.
news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.2%, the lowest it has been in the last decade. The number of jobless individuals in the workforce has decreased by over 300,000 compared to the previous quarter. Additionally, the economy has added 450,000 new jobs in various sectors, indicating a positive trend towards recovery. This decline in unemployment is seen as a promising sign for the country's economic growth and stability. Analysts predict that with the current pace, the job market is expected to continue improving in the coming months.

news.headline: Chances of US shutdown rise as Democrats oppose GOP bill.
Culture Pop Soda launches brand campaign celebrating real taste, giving away $1 million worth of soda in next year.
news.summary: Culture Pop Soda has kicked off its brand campaign for 2025, titled "If Soda Grew on Trees", which highlights the authentic and flavorful taste of their products. The campaign emphasizes that if soda were more accessible, everyone would enjoy it. To support this notion, Culture Pop Soda has pledged to distribute soda valued at 1 million dollars throughout the next year as a part of their initiative.

US Secretary of State Rubio: Panama President Mulino has taken concrete steps, distancing country from China.
Hoskinson Family Office invests $1.5M in W3i Software, Inc. for Cardano's regulated DeFi growth. Leading to new stablecoin.
news.summary: In a recent development, the Hoskinson Family Office, Inc. (HFO) based in West Lafayette, Indiana, has injected $1.5 million into W3i Software, Inc. This firm is renowned as the creator of USDM, which happens to be Cardano's initial MiCA-compliant stablecoin, backed by fiat. This substantial investment signifies a crucial move towards linking decentralized finance (DeFi) and the regulated financial sector, thereby enhancing the worldwide acceptance of Cardano. USDM, established by Moneta Digital, stands out as one of the few stablecoins that adhere to stringent regulatory guidelines.

news.headline: Veritone reported Q4 2024 results, strong topline performance, entering 2025 with momentum. Ryan Steelberg optimistic.
news.summary: Veritone, Inc. (NASDAQ: VERI), a prominent company known for its innovative AI solutions centered around human interface, recently disclosed its financial outcomes for the quarter and fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2024. The company's top-line performance in Q4 of 2024 was commendable, highlighting the promising commencement of 2025. Veritone's Chairman, President, and Chief Executive Officer, Ryan Steelberg, expressed optimism about the future citing the success of their cutting-edge Veritone Data Refinery offering. The company is particularly focused on augmenting growth within the Public Sector segment, reflecting a positive outlook for further expansion.

news.headline: Benson & Bingham wins $30M settlement in landmark premises liability case in Las Vegas, led by Joseph L. Benson II, Esq.

Benson II, Esq. spearheaded the case, showcasing the firm's commitment to advocating for injury victims and ensuring accountability for negligent property owners.

Fedewa as a new independent director, increasing their Board to eight directors, with seven being independent. news.headline: UDR Inc. declares $0.43/share quarterly dividend for Q1 of 2025, payable on April 30, marking 210 consecutive payouts.
news.summary: UDR, Inc., a prominent multifamily real estate investment trust listed as UDR on the New York Stock Exchange, has reported that its Board of Directors has approved a regular quarterly dividend for the first quarter of 2025. The dividend is set at $0.43 per share and will be disbursed in cash on April 30, 2025, to shareholders of UDR common stock recorded as of April 10, 2025. This upcoming dividend payment on April 30, 2025, will mark the 210th consecutive quarterly dividend distributed by the Company to its common stockholders.

news.headline: Operation HOPE celebrates one year of The HOPE Insider survey, with data from 2024 Q4, providing insight on low-income Americans.
news.summary: In Atlanta, Operation HOPE, a nonprofit aiding low- and moderate-income individuals in navigating the economy, unveiled the 2024 Annual Report of its widespread client survey, The HOPE Insider. This marks a year since the quarterly survey began in November 2023. The Annual Report, encompassing data up to the fourth quarter of 2024, presents a comprehensive overview based on insights gathered from thousands of participants throughout the year. Operation HOPE continues its commitment to empowering Americans economically.

news.headline: BrightView plans $100 million share repurchase program to enhance business growth and shareholder value.
news.summary: BrightView Holdings, Inc., a prominent commercial landscaping services company in the US, based in Blue Bell, Pennsylvania, declared that its Board of Directors has approved a share repurchase plan of $100 million. This move aims to enable continuous investment in the company while maintaining a balanced level of debt and allowing for strategic share buybacks. The decision is in line with the company's commitment to effectively utilizing capital and steering the growth of the business.

This move signifies a significant development in the land title insurance sector, as Pulte steps into his new role at the helm of the FHFA.

(Nasdaq: TITN), a prominent chain of full-service agricultural and construction equipment stores, revealed plans to disclose its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year concluding on January 31, 2025, on Thursday, March 20, 2025. news.headline: Portman Ridge Finance Corp. reported FY2024 net investment income of $2.59 per share. They repurchased 202,357 shares for $3.8 million during the year. They declared a quarterly base distribution of $0.47 per share for Q1 2025. The company announced a merger agreement with Logan Ridge. Total investment income for FY2024 was $62.4 million. NAV was $178.5 million as of Dec 31, 2024. Total shares repurchased were 202,357 in 2024. They entered into a merger agreement with Logan Ridge on Jan 29, 2025. The Board authorized a stock repurchase program of $10 million on Mar 12, 2025. They declared a base distribution of $0.47 per share and a supplemental distribution of $0.07 per share on Mar 13, 2025. Total investment portfolio at fair value was $405.0 million as of Dec 31, 2024, comprising 93 portfolio companies. Total stockholder distributions for 2024 amount to $2.76 per share. Management remains confident in their investment strategy and ability to drive long-term value for shareholders.
news.summary: Portman Ridge Finance Corporation reported net investment income of $2.59 per share for full year 2024 and a net asset value of $19.41 per share. They repurchased 202,357 shares totaling approximately $3.8 million in 2024. They announced a regular quarterly base distribution of $0.47 per share and a supplemental distribution of $0.07 per share for the first quarter of 2025. The company also revealed a merger agreement with Logan Ridge to enhance shareholder value. Financial highlights for the full year 2024 included total investment income of $62.4 million, core investment income of $62.2 million, and net investment income of $24.0 million. The company also plans a renewed stock repurchase program and introduced a new dividend policy for sustainable distributions across market cycles. CEO Ted Goldthorpe expressed optimism about the merger and future prospects.

news.headline: Zayo to acquire Crown Castle's Fiber Solutions business for $4.25 billion, focusing on critical fiber infrastructure for AI growth.
news.summary: Zayo, a prominent communications infrastructure provider supported by DigitalBridge and EQT, recently revealed its acquisition of Crown Castle’s Fiber Solutions business for about $4.25 billion. This move demonstrates Zayo’s commitment to enhancing its fiber infrastructure to facilitate the expansion of artificial intelligence (AI) throughout the United States and boost economic activity.

news.headline: Williams COO Michael Dunn to retire on May 2, 2025 after transforming the organization into a cohesive operating company.
news.summary: TULSA, Oklahoma - In a recent development, Williams (NYSE: WMB) has disclosed that Micheal Dunn, their executive vice president and chief operating officer, is set to retire on May 2, 2025. The President and CEO of Williams, Alan Armstrong, commended Dunn for his instrumental role in unifying the organization's operations into a top-performing entity. Under Dunn's leadership, the company transitioned from separate business units to a cohesive operational structure, emphasizing operational excellence and a strong safety ethos throughout. Armstrong expressed gratitude for Dunn's contributions to Williams' success.

news.headline: TechnipFMC CEO to speak at Piper Sandler 25th Annual Energy Conference in Las Vegas on March 18, 2025 at 8:30 a.m. PT. Webcast available.
news.summary: TechnipFMC (NYSE: FTI) revealed that Doug Pferdehirt, the Chair and CEO, is scheduled to address attendees at the upcoming Piper Sandler 25th Annual Energy Conference on March 18, 2025, at 8:30 a.m. PT in Las Vegas, Nevada. The presentation will be live-streamed on the Investor Relations website at the specified time, with no accompanying presentation materials provided. Following the event, an audio replay of the webcast will be accessible on the platform for those interested.

news.headline: Wall Street bullish Ed Yardeni cuts S&P 500 targets due to Trump's tariff threats. Best-case now 6,400 by 2025, worst case 5,800.
news.summary: One of the prominent Wall Street analysts, Ed Yardeni, known for his bullish stance, has revised his year-end price targets due to escalating risks from President Trump's tariff policies. Yardeni has adjusted his S&P 500 best-case target from 7,000 to 6,400 for the end of 2025 and from 8,000 to 7,200 for 2026. In his worst-case scenario, he predicts the index could plummet to 5,800 by the close of 2025 and 6,500 by the end of 2026. Yardeni's initial optimism has shifted, reflecting a more cautious approach as he now foresees potential challenges ahead in the market. Earlier, he had envisioned an 18% surge in the S&P 500 to reach 7,000 by the close of 2025, aligned with projections from Wells Fargo and Deutsche Bank, while Oppenheimer Asset Management had set a more ambitious 7,100 target.

news.headline: 21Shares will liquidate two bitcoin and ethereum futures ETFs as part of routine review of their product lineup.
news.summary: On March 13, 2025, 21Shares US LLC, an affiliate of 21Shares AG, a leading issuer of crypto exchange traded funds (ETFs), revealed the planned liquidation of two bitcoin futures and ethereum futures ETFs. This decision came after a routine assessment of the company’s product portfolio to guarantee its relevance to market trends, client requirements, and the evolving digital assets environment.

According to Michael Seifert, the Chairman and CEO, the year 2024 saw substantial achievements as they concentrated on involving their primary merchants and consumers, leading to a four-fold increase in revenue.

news.summary: Completes strategic assessment with a deal to vend small cell technology and fiber solutions for a total of $8.5 billion.

news.headline: Turtle Beach Corp reports record Q4 and full year 2024 results, setting new benchmarks in financial performance.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is expected to grow by 3.2% this year, down from the previous estimate of 3.6%. The decrease is attributed to ongoing trade tensions and geopolitical uncertainties. It is projected that advanced economies will grow at a rate of 1.7%, while emerging market and developing economies will see a growth rate of 4.1%. Additionally, global trade is expected to expand by 1.1% this year, a sharp decline from the 3.6% growth recorded last year. These economic developments are causing concerns among policymakers and analysts about the stability and future growth of the global economy.

news.headline: HeartSciences reports third quarter fiscal 2025 results; AI medical tech company focused on transforming ECGs for early heart disease detection.
news.summary: In Southlake, TX, on March 13, 2025, HeartSciences Inc. (Nasdaq: HSCS; HSCSW) released their financial report for the third quarter fiscal 2025 ending January 31, 2025. news.headline: Tech company releases Q4 & FY 2025 results.
news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a record low of 3.8%, the lowest it has been in the past decade. The economy has seen a significant increase in job creation with over 300,000 new jobs added in the last quarter alone. This positive trend has led to growth in various sectors including technology, healthcare, and manufacturing. news.headline: BlackRock's BUIDL fund, tokenized by Securitize, exceeds $1B in assets under management, signaling a groundbreaking achievement.

This milestone underscores Securitize's dedication to revolutionizing digital asset adoption within the institutional sector.

news.headline: Bank of America declares dividends on preferred stock for April and May 2025 in Charlotte.
news.summary: Bank of America Corporation has declared regular cash dividends for their preferred stock series. The Board of Directors approved the payout for the outstanding shares or depositary shares. The announcement was made on March 13, 2025, in Charlotte, North Carolina. news.headline: Drilling Tools Int'l Corp. reports Q4 and 2024 year-end results. Expects growth in 2025, with significant revenue increase internationally.
news.summary: Anticipating further expansion in 2025, Drilling Tools International Corp. (NASDAQ: DTI) estimates growth in consolidated revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and adjusted free cash flow. news.headline: Hyperliquid boosts margin requirements post $4M loss in liquidation. Traders need 20% collateral from March 15. $350M TLV in HLP.
news.summary: Hyperliquid, a popular platform for leveraged perpetual trading, recently faced a $4 million loss in its liquidity pool due to a trader liquidating a $200 million Ether long position. As a response, Hyperliquid has increased margin requirements, requiring traders to maintain a collateral margin of at least 20% on certain positions starting March 15. Despite the incident not being an exploit, Hyperliquid sees it as an opportunity to strengthen their margining framework. The platform has a liquidity pool with over $350 million in Total Value Locked (TVL) and has captured 70% of the market share in the perps exchange, with a daily transaction volume of around $180 million as of March 12. The platform boasts fast settlement times and low fees but is less decentralized than other exchanges.
